examples.text: يَسْمَحُ مِقْلَادٌ بِمُرُورِ التَّيَّارِ الْكَهْرَبَائِيِّ عِنْدَ إِغْلَاقِهِ وَيَمْنَعُ الْمُرُورَ عِنْدَ فَتْحِهِ
examples.english: A switch allows the passage of electric current when it is turned on and prevents its passage when it is turned off.

senses.gloss: to conquer, occupy, take possession of
to mark (a consonant) with a fatha
to open in large numbers or thoroughly

examples.text: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا وَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا عَنْهَا لَا تُفَتَّحُ لَهُمْ أَبْوَابُ ٱلسَّمَاءِ وَلَا يَدْخُلُونَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ حَتَّى يَلِجَ ٱلْجَمَلُ فِي سَمِّ ٱلْخِيَاطِ
examples.english: Indeed, those who deny Our signs and disdain them—the gates of the heaven will not be opened for them, nor shall they enter paradise until the camel passes through the needle's eye

senses.gloss: conquest, victory, an outcome; occupation of a fortified place, opening of an enemy's defenses
Fatah (a major Palestinian political party and the largest faction of the Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO))

etymology.text:
From Proto-Semitic *pataḥ-.

Cognate with Akkadian 𒁉𒌈 (pītum) and Biblical Hebrew פֶּתַח (pɛ́ṯaḥ).
